mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, elevated labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below 0.25% and an unemployment rate above 8.0% defines a liquidity trap where conventional monetary stimulus fails to restore growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with high unemployment
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE fails to add incremental predictive power over main effects (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression, or the 20-day hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-financial-crisis recovery, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a rebound in residential investment and construction employment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases during post-crisis recovery years in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending momentum, which immediately translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 52% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, rising price pressures erode real corporate margins and increase the risk of future monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production and decrease in retail sales indicates unintended inventory accumulation, which forces future production cuts and delivers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 0 and RSAFS MoM change < 0
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling RSAFS does not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms accelerating aggregate demand that lifts broad corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 vintage surprises fail to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarters during identified recovery episodes in walk-forward tests.
